body:
### Problem

On nightly, specifically on commit https://github.com/neovim/neovim/commit/5181984db990111c76d7d7bceb3d7acba7da4406, I started getting issues with my statusline, while LSP is running. Mostly typescript LS, but it randomly happened with others as well. When I stop the LSP the issue stops.

It looks like it cannot evaluate correctly which window is the active window, and then it ends up either having active window statusline on all windows, or nowhere.

I tried setting up a minimal reproduction but can't reproduce it for some reason. In my config I tried disabling almost all of the plugins, and it still happens. I'll provide it as soon as I manage to do it.
Here's a video while browsing https://github.com/neoclide/coc.nvim:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6ea56a51-4226-44aa-a8e3-f359d33923d7

### Steps to reproduce

Here's the minimal init:

```lua
vim.pack.add({
  {
    src = 'https://github.com/neovim/nvim-lspconfig',
  },
  {
    src = 'https://github.com/williamboman/mason.nvim',
  },
  {
    src = 'https://github.com/williamboman/mason-lspconfig.nvim',
  },
})
require('mason').setup()
vim.lsp.config('ts_ls', {})

require('mason-lspconfig').setup({
  ensure_installed = {
    'ts_ls',
  },
})

local function statusline_active()
  return '%#Function#ACTIVEWINDOW%*'
end

local function statusline_inactive()
  return [[ %f %m %= %y ]]
end

function _G.MyStatusline()
  local win_id = vim.fn.win_getid()
  local focus = vim.g.statusline_winid == win_id
  if focus then
    return statusline_active()
  end
  return statusline_inactive()
end

vim.o.statusline = '%!v:lua.MyStatusline()'
```

Steps to reproduce:

1. Clone https://github.com/neoclide/coc.nvim, go to it
2. `NVIM_APPNAME=statusline_test nvim -u /path/to/minimal_init.lua`, install deps, close nvim
3. Open nvim again with the above command
4. `edit src/neovim/transport/connection.ts`
5. `vs`, go to the new split
6. `edit src/configuration/configuration.ts`
7.  Do some edits in this file, check that at one moment both splits have same active statusline
8. Navigate between windows, it goes back to normal
9. Do some edits again, it gets messed up

### Expected behavior

As mentioned above, it was working fine before that commit. Here's a video with the commit before (ea8f1463ddd18cb95bb960c684aab6cd3cb4799b)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/527eaf05-2948-4a38-9566-05faa3c7020e

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

v0.13.0-dev-669+gfcd2adf8ae

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

Not applicable

### Operating system/version

Arch linux

### Terminal name/version

Kitty

### $TERM environment variable

xterm-kitty

### Installation

Bob

comment_data.body:
Do you have plugins that listen to `LspRequest` ? I don't know what you mean by "statusline window detction".

Does it happen if you remove `buf` here: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/bedfa7d58f44a8db4764027a4c82fa92f2ed60b6/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/client.lua#L725-L726

The other place where we call `nvim_exec_autocmds` with `buf` is here:

https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/bedfa7d58f44a8db4764027a4c82fa92f2ed60b6/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/client.lua#L1189-L1190


One could make an argument that `nvim_exec_autocmds` should only "filter" and shouldn't change the context of its execution. But my argument is that `nvim_exec_autocmds` is supposed to act like the *actual events*.

> Do you have plugins that listen to LspRequest ? I don't know what you mean by "statusline window detction".

Not that I know of. By "window detection" I mean that the statusline expression (`!%v:lua.statusline` format) seems to not evaluate the active window properly. If you look closely on the first video, you will see that the statusline is changing from active to inactive, even though I stay only on one window.

This is basically how I set up a statusline:

```lua
function statusline.setup()
  local win_id = vim.api.nvim_get_current_win()
  local focus = vim.g.statusline_winid == win_id
  if focus then
    return statusline_active(win_id)
  end
  return statusline_inactive()
end
vim.o.statusline = '%!v:lua.require("partials.statusline").setup()'
```

I tried removing buf on those two places, and it still happens. When I remove it on the [2nd place](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/bedfa7d58f44a8db4764027a4c82fa92f2ed60b6/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/client.lua#L1189-L1190), flickering happens a bit faster, but it's still there.

I tried narrowing it down and removing the plugins to isolate the possible issue, and it happened only when I disabled LSP. I'll give it another try tomorrow, hopefully I can figure something out.

I'm confused, it sounds like you have a config that does something with the statusline, but there are missing details here.

Just a guess, but I wonder if `g:actual_curbuf` `g:actual_curwin` aren't getting updated by `nvim_exec_autocmds` ? 

https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/5a9a357bf00abcb0d98809f97f7122887b7a4a10/runtime/doc/options.txt#L6507-L6513

Need a clearer repro

Ok I think I finally managed to set up a minimal repro:

Here's the minimal init:

```lua
vim.pack.add({
  {
    src = 'https://github.com/neovim/nvim-lspconfig',
  },
  {
    src = 'https://github.com/williamboman/mason.nvim',
  },
  {
    src = 'https://github.com/williamboman/mason-lspconfig.nvim',
  },
})
require('mason').setup()
vim.lsp.config('ts_ls', {})

require('mason-lspconfig').setup({
  ensure_installed = {
    'ts_ls',
  },
})

local function statusline_active()
  return '%#Function#ACTIVEWINDOW%*'
end

local function statusline_inactive()
  return [[ %f %m %= %y ]]
end

function _G.MyStatusline()
  local win_id = vim.fn.win_getid()
  local focus = vim.g.statusline_winid == win_id
  if focus then
    return statusline_active()
  end
  return statusline_inactive()
end

vim.o.statusline = '%!v:lua.MyStatusline()'
```

Steps to reproduce:

1. Clone https://github.com/neoclide/coc.nvim, go to it
2. `NVIM_APPNAME=statusline_test nvim -u /path/to/minimal_init.lua`, install deps, close nvim
3. Open nvim again with the above command
4. `edit src/neovim/transport/connection.ts`
5. `vs`, go to the new split
6. `edit src/configuration/configuration.ts`
7.  Do some edits in this file, check that at one moment both splits have same active statusline
8. Navigate between windows, it goes back to normal
9. Do some edits again, it gets messed up


Here's the video with minimal init:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bb3a085c-a8c9-42ad-8fc1-f18ac53e2f83



And this is with the commit before:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/95674c13-5fc1-4365-87a2-e7adfeb9eafa



Edit: Doing `:lsp stop` fixes the issue, so it must be LSP related.

Didn't check how the bisected commit affected this but yeah the only reliable way to determine the active window during statusline evaluation has been `g:actual_curwin`. Does this still work?
```lua
function() _G.MyStatusline()
  local focus = vim.api.nvim_get_current_win() == tonumber(vim.g.actual_curwin)
  ...
end
```

minimal test

```lua
vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd('FileType', {
  callback = function()
    local client = vim.lsp.start({
      name = 'ts_ls',
      cmd = { 'typescript-language-server', '--stdio' },
      root_dir = vim.uv.cwd(),
    })
    if client then
      vim.lsp.buf_attach_client(0, client)
    end
  end,
})

local function statusline_active()
  return '%#Function#ACTIVEWINDOW%*'
end

local function statusline_inactive()
  return [[ %f %m %= %y ]]
end

function _G.MyStatusline()
  local win_id = vim.fn.win_getid()
  local focus = vim.g.statusline_winid == win_id
  if focus then
    return statusline_active()
  end
  return statusline_inactive()
end

vim.o.statusline = '%!v:lua.MyStatusline()'

```

- `:e a.ts` then type `wwww`
- `:vs b.ts`

> Didn't check how the bisected commit affected this but yeah the only reliable way to determine the active window during statusline evaluation has been `g:actual_curwin`. Does this still work?
> 
> function() _G.MyStatusline()
>   local focus = vim.api.nvim_get_current_win() == tonumber(vim.g.actual_curwin)
>   ...
> end

It doesn't work. `g:actual_curwin` is nil for me in the statusline function, on both nightly and v0.12.2.

> It doesn't work. g:actual_curwin is nil for me in the statusline function

Yes sorry `g:actual_curwin` is only set for `%{` or `%{%`; `vim.o.statusline = "%{%v:lua._G.MyStatusline()%}"`...
With that syntax `g:actual_curwin` is not `nil`, but the issue is still there.

So yeah since 5181984d changes current window temporarily, I guess it should mark statuslines for redraw...
```diff
diff --git a/src/nvim/autocmd.c b/src/nvim/autocmd.c
index 940e1ed3dc..cd29b1d904 100644
--- a/src/nvim/autocmd.c
+++ b/src/nvim/autocmd.c
@@ -1463,6 +1463,8 @@ win_found:
         curwin->w_buffer = curbuf;
         curbuf->b_nwindows++;
       }
+      // redraw later in case statusline was redrawn as current
+      curwin->w_redr_status = true;

       curwin = save_curwin;
       curbuf = curwin->w_buffer;
```

Scheduling the redraw in `diagnostic.lua` also avoids the issue while being less wasteful, but probably shouldn't bother callers with this:
```diff
diff --git a/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua b/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua
index 5e0c19018c..1991724a2e 100644
--- a/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua
+++ b/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua
@@ -1145,7 +1145,9 @@ nvim_on('DiagnosticChanged', api.nvim_create_augroup('nvim.diagnostic.status', {
   desc = 'diagnostics component for the statusline',
 }, function(ev)
   if vim.fn.win_gettype(vim.fn.bufwinid(ev.buf)) == '' then
-    api.nvim__redraw({ buf = ev.buf, statusline = true })
+    vim.schedule(function()
+      api.nvim__redraw({ buf = ev.buf, statusline = true })
+    end)
   end
 end)
 ```
Minimal init is fixed for me, but in my configuration I still have the issue. I guess I'll have to find alternative ways to render different statusline per window.

we want to fix the issue fully, but need a clearer idea of what your config is doing. I understand that it's hard to create a minimal repro here, but likely just need a statusline snippet that uses whatever features coc.nvim et al are using

Yeah I know, but as you said, it's hard to make it a minimal repro. My [statusline](https://github.com/kristijanhusak/neovim-config/blob/master/nvim/lua/partials/statusline.lua#L398-L405) setup is similar to what's in the minimal repro above, but it shows more things. The statusline itself is not problematic, it's the LSP. When I remove certain autocmds on the buffer that has LSP active, like "CursorHold" to show diagnostics, the issue is less noticeable, but it's still there.

If the underlying code actually changes the buffer (and window) when triggering autocmds, I can see how this can happen. If there are some slower things happening in the autocmds, the delay in redraw can cause the flickering. More plugins overall probably cause slowness in certain areas.

I tried lualine, and it doesn't have that issue, but it handles the active/inactive window logic differently.

-----

With @glepnir [minimal init](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/40153#issuecomment-4668561985), only thing where I can trigger it to happen for a very small flicker is to do this:

1. `e a.ts`
2. `vs`
3. `e b.ts`
4. `lsp stop`

While doing `lsp stop`, keep looking on the statusline in the left window. For a very short period of time (milliseconds), you will see a flicker. This does not happen with the commit before ea8f1463ddd18cb95bb960c684aab6cd3cb4799b
Flickering is kind of expected currently without https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/40172#discussion_r3391824729 I suppose.

> Flickering is kind of expected currently without [#40172 (comment)](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/40172#discussion_r3391824729) I suppose.

Yeah, but it's more noticeable with more plugins I guess. It also still doesn't correctly re-render all windows in my case (configuration). Here's a short video with the same files as above (I know this is not helpful, just showcasing):

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7aab1756-6499-45d1-8552-07b2ba63c94c

labels.description: wrong behavior that was introduced in a previous commit (please bisect)

labels.description: issue has been tracked to a specific commit

title: Active statusline window detection randomly breaking
